prs.title: Implement RAG retrieval module and add AI Planner page
feat/deprecate subclass gh 7547
prs.body:
- **feat: optimize `populate` with `perDocumentLimit` to consolidate queries for multiple documents when possible (gh-15863)**
- **feat: add tracing support for aggregate, save, and query execution to enhance diagnostics**
- **feat: Add deprecation warnings for `Model.__subclass` and dangling models created by reusing model names with different collections, along with new tests.**

prs.title: Deprecate Model.__subclass() due to unclear behavior and dangling models (gh-7547)
prs.body:
### Problem Explanation
Subclassing a model currently allows calling `mongoose.model()` (or `connection.model()`) with an existing model name but a different collection name. This behavior:
1. Returns a model that is **not** registered in Mongoose's `models` registry (a "dangling model").
2. Relies on internal API (`Model.__subclass()`) which is undocumented and confusing.
3. Leads to implicit side effects and potential tracking issues, as these models are not easily accessible from the connection.

### Chosen Solution and Reasoning
We chose to **deprecate** `Model.__subclass()` and the associated behavior in the `model()` factory methods. 
- **Reasoning**: Maintaining multiple models with the same name on a single connection is inconsistent with Mongoose's core registry design. By deprecating this behavior, we encourage developers to use more explicit and maintainable patterns like `schema.clone()` with a unique model name or using discriminators.
- **Backward Compatibility**: Existing code will continue to function but will emit a warning, providing a clear path for migration without breaking existing applications.

### Changes
- **lib/model.js**: Added deprecation warning and JSDoc tag to `Model.__subclass()`.
- **lib/mongoose.js**: Added warning in `Mongoose.prototype.model()` when collection name mismatch creates a dangling model.
- **lib/connection.js**: Added warning in `Connection.prototype.model()` for the same dangling model scenario.
- **test/gh7547.test.js**: New test file validating that warnings are emitted as expected.

### Migration Guidance
Instead of creating a dangling model with the same name:
```javascript
const User = mongoose.model('User', schema);
const OtherUser = mongoose.model('User', 'other_users'); // Deprecated: triggers dangling model
```
Please use a unique name for the new model/collection pair:
```javascript
const User = mongoose.model('User', schema);
const OtherUser = mongoose.model('OtherUser', User.schema, 'other_users'); // Recommended
```

Fixes #7547

prs.title: feat(schema): add generateSchemaFromCollection introspection utility (gh-14570)
prs.body: This adds a new utility generateSchemaFromCollection that introspects a MongoDB collection and generates a Mongoose Schema definition. Relates to #14570. Tests included in test/introspection.test.js.

prs.title: feat(query): allow query.post() to receive query result (gh-15898)
prs.body:
## Summary
Implements #15898 - allows `query.post()` middleware to receive the query result as a parameter.

## Usage
```js
query.post(function(result) {
  if (result.deletedCount > 0) {
    // Handle successful delete
  }
});
```

## Supported signatures
- `query.post(function() {})` - backward compatible, no params
- `query.post(function(result) {})` - receives result
- `query.post(function(result, next) {})` - receives result + next for async

## Changes
- Pass `[res]` to `execPost` instead of `[]` in `Query.prototype.exec`
- Fully backward compatible
- Works with find(), findOne(), updateOne(), deleteOne(), and other operations

## Tests
Added 6 tests in `test/query.middleware.test.js` under `query.post() receives result (gh-15898)`

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

prs.title: feat: initial support for diagnostics_channel TracingChannel API
prs.body:
## Problem in current implementation
Currently, monitoring Mongoose operations from APM tools requires monkey-patching which is brittle and can lead to performance regressions.

## Objective
Enable APM tools (like OpenTelemetry) to instrument Mongoose without monkey-patching, by emitting tracing lifecycle events using diagnostics_channel. 

## Scope
Implement support for:
- Query.prototype.exec
- Aggregate.prototype.exec
- Model.prototype.save

## Features
- Follows the tracing lifecycle flow: start → operation → asyncEnd (with error on failure).
- Zero overhead when no subscribers are active.
- Contextual objects for each operation including filters, pipelines, and collection names.
- Compatible with Node.js 18+ and handles Node 20.x stable branch APIs correctly.

Note: This PR is now correctly separated from the populate-limit optimization PR.

prs.title: perf(populate): optimize perDocumentLimit to reduce query count when possible
prs.body:
## Problem in current implementation
Currently, when using perDocumentLimit, Mongoose executes one query per parent document to ensure correctness. This leads to inefficient "N+1" queries even when a single query could safely fetch all required documents.

## Optimization approach
If totalUniqueIds <= parentDocs.length * perDocumentLimit, we fetch all documents in a single query.
- Replaces perDocumentLimit with limit.
- Groups parent documents into a single query for the same model.
- Maintains backward compatibility by falling back to separate queries when the optimization condition is not met.

## Why it's safe
Mongoose's assignment logic already truncates the populated results per parent document using the limit option. By satisfying the query with one call, we gain performance without changing the effective output.

Note: This optimization is limited to non-virtual, non-count populates as referenced IDs in those cases correspond to specific documents.

prs.title: Add recursive forward forecasting function for 7-day demand prediction
Fix Fastify route registration and handler structure
prs.body:
### Summary
This PR fixes the route registration in the Fastify plugin module. Previously, the `.get()` and `.post()` methods were incorrectly chained, which is not supported. Each route is now registered separately with the proper `opts` and handler.

### Changes Made
- Separated GET and POST route registration.
- Ensured `opts` is correctly passed to both routes.
- Called `done()` after all routes are registered.
- Maintains functionality: GET and POST requests to '/' return `{ hello: 'world' }`.

### Impact
- Fixes potential runtime errors due to improper chaining.
- Keeps routes fully functional.
- Improves code readability and maintainability.

prs.title: uk.js fixed with missing translations
prs.body:
there were some fields in en.js that are missing in uk.js affecting overall user experience. i have made sure to add all the remaining fields in uk.js for better consistency. 

The missing feilds includes:
inherits
merging_column_w_inherited_definition
import_from
new_window
are_you_sure_delete_diagram
oops_smth_went_wrong
notes
show_cardinality
show_debug_coordinates
etc


this fix makes the ui more smooth by adding missing fields.

fixes #814

prs.title: docs: add Quick Start section and alternative package managers to README
prs.body: This PR improves the README by adding a Quick Start section with practical examples and installation instructions for alternative package managers (pnpm and yarn). This enhances the onboarding experience for new users.
